source: Pi session 2026-05-14 — '[Distill] Groq extraction failed: Error code: 429' at session exit
title: Distillation must fall back to Claude Haiku, then a regex heuristic, when Groq is rate-limited
what_failed: distill_session() called Groq exclusively. When the Groq free-tier TPD limit (100k tokens/day) was exhausted, the entire session's L1->L2 distillation failed. No facts from the session reached L2 — effectively all session memory was lost at exit.
where_failed: memory/pipeline.py::_extract_facts — single LLM provider, no fallback.
why_likely: Phase 6 designed for the happy path of always-available Groq. The 100k TPD limit had not been hit during dev so this was untested.
suggested_fix: Three-tier fallback chain: 1) Groq llama-3.3-70b (free, primary), 2) Claude Haiku 4.5 (~$0.001 per distill, cheap fallback), 3) regex heuristic (zero-cost last resort that catches 'my name is X', 'remember Y', 'I prefer Z' patterns). Pass anthropic_client through distill_session signature.
